notes.scope: PubMed full-history retrieval for CAR-DC, CAR-Mac, CAR-Mono, CAR-T, and CAR-NK research articles.
notes.query_strategy: Use broad_query for default high-recall retrieval, then apply downstream filtering and review routing. filtered_query remains available for narrower exploratory runs.
notes.research_article_policy: Keep original research records across clinical, observational, animal, and in vitro studies while excluding clearly non-research publication types where metadata permits.
notes.caveat: PubMed publication type metadata is imperfect. Query-time exclusion should stay conservative; downstream code must preserve auditability by retaining raw records before filtering.
notes.mesh_policy: MeSH terms are supporting evidence for topic attribution and review routing, not primary hard retrieval gates.

shared_blocks.car_concept_block: ("chimeric antigen receptor"[Title/Abstract] OR "chimeric antigen receptors"[Title/Abstract] OR "chimeric immune receptor"[Title/Abstract] OR "chimeric immune receptors"[Title/Abstract] OR "CAR"[Title/Abstract])
shared_blocks.exclude_publication_types: NOT (Review[Publication Type] OR Systematic Review[Publication Type] OR Meta-Analysis[Publication Type] OR Editorial[Publication Type] OR Comment[Publication Type] OR Letter[Publication Type] OR News[Publication Type] OR Biography[Publication Type] OR Interview[Publication Type] OR Lecture[Publication Type] OR Guideline[Publication Type] OR Practice Guideline[Publication Type] OR Consensus Development Conference[Publication Type] OR Consensus Development Conference, NIH[Publication Type] OR Historical Article[Publication Type] OR Published Erratum[Publication Type] OR Retraction of Publication[Publication Type] OR Duplicate Publication[Publication Type])
shared_blocks.exclude_title_noise: NOT (review[Title] OR editorial[Title] OR commentary[Title] OR comment[Title])

topics.label: CAR-DC
topics.description: CAR-engineered dendritic cells and dendritic-cell-derived CAR platforms.
topics.query_notes: Dendritic-cell terminology is variable, so combine exact CAR-DC phrasings with broader CAR concept plus dendritic-cell concept logic.
must_hit_examples: Representative CAR dendritic cell engineering papers should be manually checked after each query revision.
Any paper explicitly using the phrase CAR-DC in title or abstract must be captured.
topics.broad_query: ("CAR-DC"[Title/Abstract] OR "CAR-DCs"[Title/Abstract] OR "CAR DC"[Title/Abstract] OR "CAR DCs"[Title/Abstract] OR "CAR dendritic"[Title/Abstract] OR "CAR dendritic cell"[Title/Abstract] OR "CAR dendritic cells"[Title/Abstract] OR "CAR-engineered dendritic cell"[Title/Abstract] OR "CAR-engineered dendritic cells"[Title/Abstract] OR "CAR-modified dendritic cell"[Title/Abstract] OR "CAR-modified dendritic cells"[Title/Abstract] OR "CAR-transduced dendritic cell"[Title/Abstract] OR "CAR-transduced dendritic cells"[Title/Abstract] OR "dendritic cell CAR"[Title/Abstract] OR "dendritic cells CAR"[Title/Abstract] OR "dendritic cell expressing chimeric antigen receptor"[Title/Abstract] OR "dendritic cells expressing chimeric antigen receptors"[Title/Abstract] OR "chimeric antigen receptor dendritic cell"[Title/Abstract] OR "chimeric antigen receptor dendritic cells"[Title/Abstract])
topics.filtered_query: (("CAR-DC"[Title/Abstract] OR "CAR-DCs"[Title/Abstract] OR "CAR DC"[Title/Abstract] OR "CAR DCs"[Title/Abstract] OR "CAR dendritic"[Title/Abstract] OR "CAR dendritic cell"[Title/Abstract] OR "CAR dendritic cells"[Title/Abstract] OR "CAR-engineered dendritic cell"[Title/Abstract] OR "CAR-engineered dendritic cells"[Title/Abstract] OR "CAR-modified dendritic cell"[Title/Abstract] OR "CAR-modified dendritic cells"[Title/Abstract] OR "CAR-transduced dendritic cell"[Title/Abstract] OR "CAR-transduced dendritic cells"[Title/Abstract] OR "dendritic cell CAR"[Title/Abstract] OR "dendritic cells CAR"[Title/Abstract] OR "dendritic cell expressing chimeric antigen receptor"[Title/Abstract] OR "dendritic cells expressing chimeric antigen receptors"[Title/Abstract] OR "chimeric antigen receptor dendritic cell"[Title/Abstract] OR "chimeric antigen receptor dendritic cells"[Title/Abstract])) NOT (Review[Publication Type] OR Systematic Review[Publication Type] OR Meta-Analysis[Publication Type] OR Editorial[Publication Type] OR Comment[Publication Type] OR Letter[Publication Type] OR News[Publication Type] OR Biography[Publication Type] OR Interview[Publication Type] OR Lecture[Publication Type] OR Guideline[Publication Type] OR Practice Guideline[Publication Type] OR Consensus Development Conference[Publication Type] OR Consensus Development Conference, NIH[Publication Type] OR Historical Article[Publication Type] OR Published Erratum[Publication Type] OR Retraction of Publication[Publication Type] OR Duplicate Publication[Publication Type]) NOT (review[Title] OR editorial[Title] OR commentary[Title] OR comment[Title])

primary_title_abstract_phrases: dendritic cell expressing chimeric antigen receptor
dendritic cells expressing chimeric antigen receptors

topics.label: CAR-Mac
topics.description: CAR macrophages and CAR-engineered macrophage studies.
topics.query_notes: Macrophage studies use both explicit CAR-Mac phrasing and broader engineered-macrophage language.
must_hit_examples: Any paper explicitly using CAR macrophage or CAR-Mac in title or abstract must be captured.
Representative CAR macrophage translational studies should be checked during validation.

topics.label: CAR-Mono
topics.description: CAR monocytes and CAR-engineered monocyte studies.
topics.query_notes: Monocyte terminology is less standardized, so broad_query keeps a wider monocyte and monocytic vocabulary.
must_hit_examples: Any paper explicitly using CAR monocyte or CAR-Mono in title or abstract must be captured.
Validation should inspect overlap with macrophage and dendritic-cell papers.

topics.label: CAR-T
topics.description: CAR T-cell studies across the full PubMed history.
topics.query_notes: CAR-T requires high recall and explicit support for earlier terminology such as chimeric immune receptor and T-body.
must_hit_examples: Early foundational CAR T-cell papers should be maintained as sentinel validation records.
Any paper explicitly using CAR-T or chimeric antigen receptor T cell in title or abstract must be captured.

topics.label: CAR-NK

topics.query_notes: CAR-NK literature mixes exact CAR-NK phrasing with broader natural-killer-cell engineering language.
must_hit_examples: Any paper explicitly using CAR-NK or chimeric antigen receptor NK cell in title or abstract must be captured.
Validation should include early and recent CAR NK engineering papers.
